title: Where Does Cycling Rank on the Outdoor Rec GDP?
content:
For many of us, riding bikes is our world. But we still recreate in other ways. Be it backpacking, thru-hiking, trail running, rafting, skiing, hunting, fishing, or car camping, there are many facets to Outdoor Recreation. Kyle Frost, who used to work at Mountain Gazette, one of our favorite print publications, released the latest Outdoor Rec data, and it’s fascinating.

“Every year, the Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) puts out a report on the Outdoor Recreation Satellite Account (OREC), diving into the economic impact of the industry. This year’s headline is: $1.3 trillion in gross output, $696.7 billion in value added, 2.4% of GDP, 5.2 million jobs. Those are the numbers you’ll see in all the press releases and advocacy materials for the next 12 months. They’re real numbers, but they deserve a bit more context.”

We’re glad to see cycling on the chart, but man, it sure puts things into perspective.

Read more at Kyle Frost’s new venture, Here and There.

The post Where Does Cycling Rank on the Outdoor Rec GDP? appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
